spec_match.rationale: The demo mounts a real <usermedia> element, probes HTMLUserMediaElement, configures it with setConstraints(), and listens for stream/error/cancel with a clearly labelled getUserMedia fallback.

interactive.rationale: Users can activate the native element when supported or its fallback button when not, then stop the resulting stream and inspect track counts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The copy frames <usermedia> as a browser-controlled, user-activated media capture control rather than a custom element or generic permission control.

edge_cases.rationale: Unsupported browsers, media errors, cancellation, and stream cleanup are surfaced without uncaught failures.

references.rationale: The page includes the correct chromestatus.com/feature/4926233538330624 reference.

design_system.rationale: The page uses the shared visual system, contrast-safe variables, and real controls rather than a static code card.

summary: The facade was replaced with a native <usermedia> mount, correct support detection, stream/error/cancel events, and an honest getUserMedia fallback path.
